title: Bridge Essay: Cognitive-affective neural plasticity following active-controlled mindfulness in

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Form/Meditation** (Phase 7) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Form/Meditation to mechanistic neuroscience through Cognitive-affective, neural, plasticity, following, active-controlled.

**Cognitive-affective neural plasticity following active-controlled mindfulness intervention.** — J Neurosci. PMID: 23115195. DOI: 10.1523/JNEUROSCI.2957-12.2012. Mindfulness meditation is aset of attention-based, regulatory, and self-inquiry training regimes. Although the impact of mindfulness training (MT) on self-regulation is well established, the neural mechanisms supporting such plasticity are poorly understood. MT is thought to act through interoceptive salience and attentional control mechanisms, but until now conflicting evidence from behavioral and neural measures renders difficult distinguishing their respective roles. To resolve this question
